svg.text: Radio Oracle — Jetson Orin Nano Wiring
RGB LED · Momentary Action Button · Power Toggle Switch · Volume Potentiometer (via ADS1115 I²C ADC)
Pin numbers are BCM (matches config/settings.py); header position numbers shown in parentheses

g.text: color encodes mode (radio / librarian / thinking / error)
long press (≥1 s): enter Librarian mode
closed = device on (radio active) · open = standby
• 1 × 8 mm common-cathode RGB LED (4-lead) + 3 × 330 Ω resistor (¼ W)
• 1 × momentary push-button — short = next, long ≥1 s = Librarian mode
• 1 × SPST toggle switch — closed wakes the device; open puts it in standby
• 1 × 10 kΩ linear potentiometer + 1 × ADS1115 I²C ADC breakout
Common-anode RGB LED works too, but invert the GPIO drive (active-LOW) and tie the common pin to 3.3 V.
USB DAC plugs into a Jetson USB-A port (no GPIO wiring) — see the labelled USB-A stack.
